note: accept: sub-LN/sandwich is the interior of the pre/post-norm axis — pre-norm (baseline) and post-norm (closed) are the corners. Clean identity-init at step 0 (γ=1, β=0 collapse algebraically). Sharp bet: pre-norm drift grows residual scale across depth, LN_post bounds each sublayer's contribution. Null partitions with 016-qk-norm: 016 is per-head logit (attention site), 017 is per-sublayer output (residual site). Both at 6 layers are depth-stability probes; expected null is informative.

accept: mechanism faithful (LN_post wraps sublayer output after layerscale, before residual add in both pre-norm and post-norm branches), single-bool default OFF, no HP drift, identity-safe. Plan cost-claim math is off (2*d_model not d_model²) but doesn't gate A/B.
